comment.body:
Hi Alma, I have a list of questions I wrote down this week and if you can answer some or all of them when you have the time, I’d be very grateful. Thank you for all you do, you’ve completely changed the way I look at markets forever 🙏

- when you say skew is tested in your daily analysis, do you simply mean the regular skew that can be pulled from an exchange like IBKR? And if so (or if not so) how are you calculating an X % increase/decreased on the put or call skew? Do you have an exact number it outputs on the chain or is it the degree of the slope that needs to change?  

- I’m working with the OD team and using data from their API they just released for my own modeling (can’t thank you enough for bringing that to us) and I found out that the CBOE volume originator data isn’t actually positioning data. You obviously are able to chart it and I’m just wondering what datasets I need to model something like that too or if it is its own dataset on CBOE (I can’t seem to find it). They said it’s expensive which I can understand why but I’m just wondering the datasets are so I can use them in the future

- What are the dynamics that cause vomma to create caps and floors on price and volume like what you talked about in the weekly post? Phenomenal read because the cap on price was SPOT ON this week 

- Also, why is vanna more of a medium term timeframe Greeks and vomma more of a nearer term? What causes that to be the case? I also notice you almost never really talk about charm, is that because it doesn’t really have that large of an impact or do MMs hedge it so efficiently that it’s negligible? 

- I remember you had said in the past that Greeks aren’t everything but I cannot for the life of me find what you had said was more important. So I have to ask you, what is your ranking of the most important concepts to apply to financial market in your opinion? From most important to least important on how they impact price?

comment.body: Hi @Alma  trying ask few questions but unable to dm.
comment.raw_body: Hi ${1}  trying ask few questions but unable to dm.

comment.body:
1) the best meassure of skew is the 25d put minus 25d call, normalized to ATM vol. it is basically the slope, yes
I use 22 deltas. 
similarly with the tails.
use fix strike skew and monitor the difference in % 
(I purposely don't use 'skew sold' or 'skew bought' expressions, bcs many ppl would be confused again... because sold skew = shorter skew = put IV down, bough skew = longer skew = call IV down)

2) first you need this https://datashop.cboe.com/cboe-options-open-close-volume-summary
, but you should contant them, and they will tailor your request to your needs and possibilities..

3) it is the "tail wags the dog" effect, bcs volatility can be traded, and so the wall street can directly influence the vol of vol.
what is VIX? the IV of the next 30 days SPX options. so when you trade with VIX options, you trade with the vanna and vomma of the next 30 days SPX options, bcs options trading is volatility trading too, and as a result of supply/demand + dealer flows the convexities or concavities can be artificially flattened or steepened. 
So, for example if an MM has long vanna and long vomma exposure, whats gonna happen with the curve if vol goes up? the vega almost-bell curve will elevate at the 10-20 deltas, consequently the vanna curve will flatten (bcs vanna = vega to spot), so the more vol goes up, the less delta the MM will get to hedge, and as a result the realized log returns will start to lessen relatively. 
or from the perspective of vega, the more spot goes down (long vanna environment) when vol catched up, the less his vega exposure will change = less and less additional vega to hedge leading to less vol bid.
this is why vomma handles the realized kurtosis of the distribution.

4) charm matters around the expo itself... that passive selling is not too exciting to be honest. after a lot of backtest, betting on charm is worse than a coinflip. no edge.
color is more important in my read. 

5) many ppl here recognized that the premarket OD heatmap (the one without the 0DTE updates) is more accurate than the 10 min updated one. Do you know why? bcs the traders trade the structure!
and on the not-updated OD chart you see exactly the structure that the traders are betting on. the 0DTE update is mostly noise, positioning shifts very fast. 
this is why a good volume profile is also enough for intraday trading. (no vol trading then tho)
knowing how to read the sentiment, how to derive the structure and the distribution + knowing about delta neutral and also momentum strategies is what really matters. 
Positioning is a good source of info... i can derive the distribution from it, including volatility. better edge. but the overemphatize of dealer flow is just a salesman strategy for many, like if it would be the 'hidden force' that drives the markets... no free lunch, market is stochastic. and dealer flow is a double negative: i buy a call - dealer short the call (short delta) - dealer buys futs to hedge
comment.raw_body:
1) the best meassure of skew is the 25d put minus 25d call, normalized to ATM vol. it is basically the slope, yes
I use 22 deltas. 
similarly with the tails.
use fix strike skew and monitor the difference in % 
(I purposely don't use 'skew sold' or 'skew bought' expressions, bcs many ppl would be confused again... because sold skew = shorter skew = put IV down, bough skew = longer skew = call IV down)

2) first you need this https://datashop.cboe.com/cboe-options-open-close-volume-summary
, but you should contant them, and they will tailor your request to your needs and possibilities..

3) it is the "tail wags the dog" effect, bcs volatility can be traded, and so the wall street can directly influence the vol of vol.
what is VIX? the IV of the next 30 days SPX options. so when you trade with VIX options, you trade with the vanna and vomma of the next 30 days SPX options, bcs options trading is volatility trading too, and as a result of supply/demand + dealer flows the convexities or concavities can be artificially flattened or steepened. 
So, for example if an MM has long vanna and long vomma exposure, whats gonna happen with the curve if vol goes up? the vega almost-bell curve will elevate at the 10-20 deltas, consequently the vanna curve will flatten (bcs vanna = vega to spot), so the more vol goes up, the less delta the MM will get to hedge, and as a result the realized log returns will start to lessen relatively. 
or from the perspective of vega, the more spot goes down (long vanna environment) when vol catched up, the less his vega exposure will change = less and less additional vega to hedge leading to less vol bid.
this is why vomma handles the realized kurtosis of the distribution.

4) charm matters around the expo itself... that passive selling is not too exciting to be honest. after a lot of backtest, betting on charm is worse than a coinflip. no edge.
color is more important in my read. 

5) many ppl here recognized that the premarket OD heatmap (the one without the 0DTE updates) is more accurate than the 10 min updated one. Do you know why? bcs the traders trade the structure!
and on the not-updated OD chart you see exactly the structure that the traders are betting on. the 0DTE update is mostly noise, positioning shifts very fast. 
this is why a good volume profile is also enough for intraday trading. (no vol trading then tho)
knowing how to read the sentiment, how to derive the structure and the distribution + knowing about delta neutral and also momentum strategies is what really matters. 
Positioning is a good source of info... i can derive the distribution from it, including volatility. better edge. but the overemphatize of dealer flow is just a salesman strategy for many, like if it would be the 'hidden force' that drives the markets... no free lunch, market is stochastic. and dealer flow is a double negative: i buy a call - dealer short the call (short delta) - dealer buys futs to hedge

comment.body:
the reason it looks like it works is because the market shows a log growth pattern, and fibonacci ratios are close to the log spiral ratios. mathematically the fibonacci spiral is teh discrete approximation of the log spiral. 
this creates teh VISUAL illusion that the fibonacci retracement tool works. 
but if you backtest it, it will give you a poor result.

the reason it looks like it works is because the market shows a log growth pattern, and fibonacci ratios are close to the log spiral ratios. mathematically the fibonacci spiral is teh discrete approximation of the log spiral. 
this creates teh VISUAL illusion that the fibonacci retracement tool works. 
but if you backtest it, it will give you a poor result.

comment.body:
for my strategy it doesn't matter if i get one or not, you could have read this in the 2nd part of the trading principles post.
this thing can hit 7150 before giving a decent pullback... really dunno. 

Imagine you play chess with a cheater. You almost give him a mate, then suddenly he takes his king down from the table and puts it next to the table. 
now you cannot mate him. 
how to win such a game?
or... even better... how to analyse such a game? 
thats what happening.
